{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 1.0,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.3,
    "R1.4": 0.3,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.0,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.3,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "To “make this stronger,” should the focus be (A) language polish and clarity only while preserving all claims, (B) tighter framing and argumentation with permission to adjust or hedge claims (e.g., the “first” assertions) without adding citations, or (C) both (B) plus assessing novelty against named prior work (requires providing key references)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Strengthening likely hinges on sharpening the thesis and contribution framing, especially around the claimed novelty and problem stakes.",
    "R1.2": "Ensuring the necessity of commitment reliability and how the constrained RL approach addresses it follows a tight, defensible argument chain is central.",
    "R1.6": "Conciseness, emphasis, and tone can make the abstract more forceful and readable without altering content.",
    "RX.1": "Claims such as being the “first” and achieving “90%” require appropriate qualifiers or context to disclose uncertainty and conditions.",
    "RX.2": "We must avoid overstating novelty or generality beyond what the excerpt supports.",
    "RX.3": "Work should remain confined to strengthening the provided abstract text and not expand scope into new experiments or extended literature surveys unless requested.",
    "RX.4": "Wording should not encourage unsafe operational practices or misrepresent reliability implications for market participation.",
    "RX.5": "Any changes should maintain traceability to the original assertions so that edits are attributable and verifiable."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: To “make this stronger,” should the focus be (A) language polish and clarity only while preserving all claims, (B) tighter framing and argumentation with permission to adjust or hedge claims (e.g., the “first” assertions) without adding citations, or (C) both (B) plus assessing novelty against named prior work (requires providing key references)?

rationale.R1.1: Strengthening likely hinges on sharpening the thesis and contribution framing, especially around the claimed novelty and problem stakes.
rationale.R1.2: Ensuring the necessity of commitment reliability and how the constrained RL approach addresses it follows a tight, defensible argument chain is central.
rationale.R1.6: Conciseness, emphasis, and tone can make the abstract more forceful and readable without altering content.
rationale.RX.1: Claims such as being the “first” and achieving “90%” require appropriate qualifiers or context to disclose uncertainty and conditions.
rationale.RX.2: We must avoid overstating novelty or generality beyond what the excerpt supports.
rationale.RX.3: Work should remain confined to strengthening the provided abstract text and not expand scope into new experiments or extended literature surveys unless requested.
rationale.RX.4: Wording should not encourage unsafe operational practices or misrepresent reliability implications for market participation.
rationale.RX.5: Any changes should maintain traceability to the original assertions so that edits are attributable and verifiable.
